programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy faces the vicious claws of a troubled cat that is terrorising a family about to bring home a newborn baby. He meets an inspirational feline named Skinny

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps an old friend recruit a new generation of volunteers in an effort to save community cats in Philadelphia

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Gerry investigates a situation involving stray bullets while checking teal hunters along the border fence. Turtle hatchlings are released on South Padre Island.

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: A dog with a rare disease needs life-saving surgery - an operation Dr Jeff has never performed before. The vets also treat a cat with a mysterious injury

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson makes a treehouse for American football player Antonio Brown, who wants a window wall that overlooks a basketball court and football field

programme.desc: Former wrestler Kurt comes to terms with his fear of bees, Tameeka combats her hatred of millipedes, and Shelly confronts a long-standing unease relating to sharks

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Otto and Charlotte face jeopardy on the seas as they take part in an urgent rescue mission to Halibut Cove

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil seeks help from McCarthys unlikeliest resident as he pursues his mine tour project, and Tim gets a reminder of home when Mark pays him a surprise visit

programme.title: Deadly Islands
programme.desc: Biologist and predator specialist Dave Salmoni journeys to an island in the South Pacific to investigate why its waters are home to a large concentration of sharks

programme.title: Project Grizzly
programme.desc: Jeff is pleased with Bob and Screech learning how to feed themselves. He also teaches them how to den up for the winter - and the bears get territorial when Jeffs friend arrives

programme.title: Richard E Grants Seven Deadly Sins
programme.desc: Richard E Grant explores the evolutionary roots of greed - the act of taking too much. From hamsters, to baboons, do animals also display this seemingly human trait?

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Out on a Limb
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his team are challenged with creating a lighthouse treehouse that sits atop a seawall along the coast of Washington, but face a logistical nightmare along the way

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: Members of the team rescue a cat trapped in a drain deep underground, and help an abandoned dog in danger of freezing to death

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: The vets try to save a Doberman Pinschers leg after it is found with its nails growing into the pads of its feet

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates the behaviour of cat Einstein, which is mild until it sees fellow feline Izzy - and then goes on the attack. Plus, an expectant couple worry about their pets mood swings

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: Exploring the birthplace of the domestic shorthair. Plus, a look at how many breeds compete at Cat Fanciers Association-sponsored shows, and the prime feline task in New York

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Game Wardens race to confront reckless jet skiers wreaking havoc at a boat ramp. Plus, a crabber turns hostile when caught without a fishing licence

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Denver vet Dr Jeff helps an injured tiger at a wild animal sanctuary. Dr Amy tackles exploratory surgery on a cat with a strange appetite

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff operates on a dog rushed in after a growth on its neck ruptures. The team performs a risky procedure on a pair of rescued miniature donkeys

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: When an aggressive dog is in danger of being put down, Tia heads to South Carolina to save him. Plus, parolee Toney Converse takes on more responsibility

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Villalobos prepares for Hurricane Barry with Tia and the crew urgently ensuring the animals safety. A cancer survivor hopes to rescue an unwanted dog

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy visits a feline that works with its guardian to help cats with special needs find forever homes. He also learns about cats roles in a prison programme

programme.title: Life at Vet University
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the University of Pennsylvania School of Veterinary Medicine. School is in full swing and in just a few months the students will be doctors - but first they must first pass their final rotations

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and the team build a charming treehouse in the style of a rustic river shack along the banks of the Mississippi

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: Dog owners make over their poodles to compete in grooming contests, and there is a feature on a household of domesticated giraffes in Kenya

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: The process that caterpillars go through to transform into magnificent butterflies while in their cocoon. Plus, the facts about cats tongues

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Why some cats are cross-eyed, how meerkats are able to eat poisonous scorpions and hippos ability to sleep while holding their breath

programme.title: Extinct or Alive: The Lost Shark
programme.desc: Wildlife biologist Forrest Galante is in the Maldives in search of the supposedly extinct Pondicherry shark, with sightings in the area leading many to believe it is alive

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: The harsh conditions begin to take their toll on the volunteers, as they experience a blizzard, freezing rivers and sub-zero temperatures, but know they cannot turn back

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King look at some bizarre client requests in which they had to go the extra mile to get the job done - not that it stopped the pair of passionate designers

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Wayde King and Brett Raymer have their creativity put to the test when a client requests an eight-piece drum set. Plus, a bar owner wants a tank with a lava-emitting volcano

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: Lake Windsor on Inagua Island is home to a mystery. How did giant ocean-dwelling fish get there? The team aims to find out - and catch some bonefish

programme.title: Project Grizzly
programme.desc: As the bears training nears its end, Jeff has one lesson left to teach - fear of humans. The release date is imminent, but will they be ready to survive alone?

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Deep in Kentucky, Mireya and Ronny encounter unidentified creatures responding to the recording of a Bigfoot howl

programme.title: Extinct or Alive: The Lost Shark
programme.desc: Wildlife biologist Forrest Galante is in the Maldives in search of the supposedly extinct Pondicherry shark, with sightings in the area leading many to believe it is alive

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Psychologist Dr Zasio tries to help temper the phobias of Sandra, who believes that cats are evil, Shereese, who is terrified of dogs and cure Patricks crippling fear of spiders

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Word Up! Black American Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of BBC performances by African-American artists of the 1980s, featuring Salt-N-Pepa, Cameo, Luther Vandross, Doug E Fresh, Whitney Houston, Chaka Khan, Shalamar and Kid Creole

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the hidden villages of London. Despite many rural settlements like Hornsey and Dagenham being swallowed up by the expansion of London, Hampstead residents successfully fought to preserve their village heritage, while in recent years Londoners have created a new breed of urban villages like Crouch End and Walthamstow

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank and the Family That Built Gothic Britain
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the changing fortunes of a family of architects, following the rise and fall of George Gilbert Scott, his son George Junior, and grandson Giles. Presenter Dan Cruickshank finds out how the dynasty was responsible for the creation of iconic landmarks such as the Midland Hotel at St Pancras station, and helped shape the Victorian age with their designs

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast September 10, 1999. Including performances from Shaft, Enrique Iglesias, Shania Twain, The Offspring, and Lou Bega

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Emma Ledden presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast September 17, 1999. Featuring performances from Sixpence None The Richer, Thunderbugs, The All-seeing I Feat Phil Oakey, Lyte Funky Ones, and Vengaboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Richard Skinner and Janice Long introduce performances by Culture Club, the Human League, FR David, Twisted Sister, Toto, Heaven 17, Thompson Twins, Spandau Ballet and David Bowie

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the chart show from April 21, 1994, featuring Crash Test Dummies, Bitty McLean, CJ Lewis, the Pretenders, Erasure, Crystal Waters, Deacon Blue, Toni Di Bart and Prince

programme.title: Prince and His Songs at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive appearances on the BBC by the musician as well as performances by artists who covered his songs over the years, including Beyonce, Muse, Tom Jones, the Bangles, Pink, Alicia Keys, Michael Kiwanuka, Chaka Khan and Sinead OConnor

programme.title: Prince and the Revolution: Live
programme.desc: A 1985 performance by the American musician and his band from the Purple Rain tour at the Syracuse Carrier Dome in New York, remixed, remastered and visually enhanced. The film features hits from the popular albums Purple Rain, Controversy and 1999, including an 18-minute version of Purple Rain

programme.title: Black Music Legends of the 1980s
programme.desc: Profile of the American musician, who rose to fame in the 1980s with hits including 1999, Kiss and Raspberry Beret. As well as achieving significant commercial success around the world, he won critical acclaim for his adventurous, genre-blurring albums, such as Sign O the Times and Around the World in a Day - while also making headlines for his sexually explicit lyrics and stage shows, as well as his legal battle to retain control of his name and music. Featuring contributions by guitarist Dez Dickerson, Public Enemy frontman Chuck D, soul singer Beverley Knight and Paisley Park label president Alan Leeds

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast September 10, 1999. Including performances from Shaft, Enrique Iglesias, Shania Twain, The Offspring, and Lou Bega

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Emma Ledden presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast September 17, 1999. Featuring performances from Sixpence None The Richer, Thunderbugs, The All-seeing I Feat Phil Oakey, Lyte Funky Ones, and Vengaboys

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Richard Skinner and Janice Long introduce performances by Culture Club, the Human League, FR David, Twisted Sister, Toto, Heaven 17, Thompson Twins, Spandau Ballet and David Bowie

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the chart show from April 21, 1994, featuring Crash Test Dummies, Bitty McLean, CJ Lewis, the Pretenders, Erasure, Crystal Waters, Deacon Blue, Toni Di Bart and Prince

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Catrin Heledd presents live coverage of the evening session on day one at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Zhao Xintongs first-round match against a qualifier, and the start of Barry Hawkins opener. Kyren Wilson was in Zhaos position as defending champion last year and he was edged out 10-9 by Lei Peifan in the first round, with Lee becoming the first player to overcome a defending champion in his debut match at the Crucible since Stuart Bingham defeated Stephen Hendry at the 2000 event. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: The Crucible: 40 Golden Snooker Years
programme.desc: Steve Davis celebrates the World Snooker Championships time at the Sheffield venue, talking to some of the players forever ingrained in the sports history. The Crucible has become the home of snooker since the championships moved there in 1977, helping to create memorable moments and scenes of drama that live on to this day. Featuring interviews with former champions Stephen Hendry and Dennis Taylor, six-time runner-up and fans favourite Jimmy White, and celebrity snooker addicts Richard Osman and Stephen Fry

programme.title: On Cue with Steve Davis: 1985 World Championship Final
programme.desc: Steve Davis presents a look at the classic 1985 World Snooker Championship Final, also known as the black ball final, between himself and Dennis Taylor. The match took place at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, England, and had over 18 million viewers watching as the two battled past midnight. First broadcast 2 November 1998

programme.title: The Promised Land (2023)
programme.desc: In 18th-century Denmark, war hero and retired army captain Ludvig Kahlen fights to settle on and cultivate uninhabitable heathland on the remote Jutland peninsula in the name of the king. Period drama, starring Mads Mikkelsen. In Danish, Swedish, Norwegian and German

programme.title: Discovering the Music of Antiquity
programme.desc: The discovery of an ancient music score deep within the storerooms of the Louvre leads musical historians on a mission to realise its potential, sung through a modern arrangement. But to hear the piece as the Greeks did 2,400 years ago, it has to be played on period instruments. This documentary follows the journey from the Greek cities of Anatolia to the Ptolemies Egypt, from the mythical site of Delphi to discoveries made in Pompeii, where archaeological excavations have unearthed instrument remains

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans a candlelit supper to impress Emmet, the director of the local amateur operatic society, with her singing talents. But fate intervenes and the event does not go as expected. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and David Griffin

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy makes plans to seduce Veronica at his bring-a-bottle party, though he must first get his parents to go on a second honeymoon. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: The Crucible: 40 Golden Snooker Years
programme.desc: Steve Davis celebrates the World Snooker Championships time at the Sheffield venue, talking to some of the players forever ingrained in the sports history. The Crucible has become the home of snooker since the championships moved there in 1977, helping to create memorable moments and scenes of drama that live on to this day. Featuring interviews with former champions Stephen Hendry and Dennis Taylor, six-time runner-up and fans favourite Jimmy White, and celebrity snooker addicts Richard Osman and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Neglected Birds Found and Highland Blaze Seen from Space
programme.desc: An emergency in Hertfordshire uncovers a case of animal neglect and in the Scottish Highlands, fire crews battle a wildfire so large its visible from space. Paul Colgan takes part in first aid training catered specifically to farmers in Dumfries

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin finds himself dodging spiders at a house in Coventry. Owain Wyn Evans is in Wales, where a detached house has a dodgy extension, and Martin Roberts is in Bristol, swooning over a 1930s semi with bags of character

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts David Harper and Irita Marriott give advice to two teams at a packed antiques fair in the Scottish Borders town of Kelso, while presenter Roo Irvine meets a man who makes famous landmarks out of matchsticks

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown discovers theres more drama offstage than on in the latest production by the Kembleford Players. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown guides a couple who are looking for the peace and quiet Bedfordshire has to offer, and also visits Biggleswade to find out about an easily accessible off-road, mult-iuser path that circumnavigates the market town,

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: After giving them business-building action plans at his makers bootcamp, Dominic Chinea catches up with textile weaver Shiv from Brighton and portrait artist Jill from Berkshire to find out how they have got on

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which George, Beth, Joyce, Matthew and Ria race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and JB Gill present the magazine-style programme with usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: Unza Saleem is given the task of painting brothers Jordan and Cian, who carry a faulty gene that means they will almost certainly develop symptoms of dementia in their forties. However, just as Unza is getting to grips with the portrait, she has her own family emergency that threatens to derail the project entirely

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigates when a seaweed farm is targeted in an act of sabotage, with fisherman Jeff Simmons swearing that he saw the legendary mermaid of Shipton Abbott on the night of the incident. With no logical explanation for how someone could damage the farm and get away unseen among glowing bioluminescent waters, Esther braces herself for an excited Humphrey to run wild with the possibility that their prime suspect could be a mermaid

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Sports presenter Gabby Logan hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedians Miles Jupp and Michelle Wolf joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conors one true love Linda Walsh is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor Phil Dunster, who rose to fame in hit show Ted Lasso, to talk about his new comedy Rooster. Actress Cush Jumbo chats about the second season of crime thriller Criminal Record, and comedian Josh Widdicombe discusses his podcast The Museum of Pop Culture, while Dan Levy, creator, writer and star of the multi-award winning show Schitts Creek, discusses the series

programme.title: The Royal Hotel (2023)
programme.desc: American best friends Hanna and Liv run out of money in Sydney as they backpack around Australia. The cash-strapped twentysomethings seek temporary employment to pay for the rest of the trip. However, after taking a job in a pub, they are confronted by the unruly locals. Thriller, starring Julia Garner, Jessica Henwick, Hugo Weaving and Ursula Yovich

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Nathan Outlaw, Andi Oliver and Sophia Handschuh and guest Judi Love, while drinks expert Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin creates a drum beat on an old kitchen sink in Stoke and there are ambitious plans for a period flat in Stirling and a car park in Leeds

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Alex Scott and guests look ahead to the weekends matches, including Manchester City v Arsenal. Plus, news from the lunchtime game between Brentford and Fulham

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian presents from Harrogate in North Yorkshire, where he learns about a scheme to recycle items from landfill. Two red and blue teams battle it out, with help from experts Izzie Balmer and John Cameron, before heading to the auction

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Live coverage of the afternoon session on day one at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the opening frames of Mark Williams and Ziao Guodongs respective first-round matches against qualifiers. Williams withstood a barrage of 466 unanswered points from Wu Yize to end last years corresponding session 5-4 ahead. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the results and this afternoons news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents a celebrity edition of the high stakes quiz, with Amy Dowden, Nina Wadia, Montell Douglas and Toyah Willcox playing to win money for their chosen charities. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Rhod Gilbert, Jill Scott, Aisling Bea, Guz Khan, Nitro and the Rev Kate Bottley

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: Lewis Capaldi bravely hands over his phone in Send to All before taking to the stage to perform his number one single Forget Me. One lucky football fans boyhood dream comes true as he unknowingly signs for his team, and theres a feisty performance from our Unexpected Star of the Show

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Celebrity edition of the quiz, hosted by Romesh Ranganathan. Mel B, Eamonn Holmes, Shobna Gulati, Denis Lawson, Arielle Free, Briony May Williams, Nick Pickard and Rob Bent are the contestants battling it out to be crowned the strongest link and will take home the prize for their charity

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The team gathers for an emotional funeral, while a desperate race is underway to ensure Faith makes it to the ED in time to give birth, but complications ensue during labour. Flynn informs Matty that the clinical fellowship is his if he wants it, and is confused when he doesnt seem pleased. Matty questions whether theyd be having this conversation if Kim was still here

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee borrows a 30-year-old campervan from his cousin to take Lucy away for the weekend. Noting that it has seen better days, she is less than impressed. Before she can make plans to relocate to a local hotel, there is a knock at the door and a neighbouring camper barges in for a cup of tea. However, its not just a drink this strange woman wants, as Lee and Lucy discover when she shares her deepest, darkest secrets. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Felicity Montagu

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Chelsea v Manchester United at Stamford Bridge and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton and Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Chelsea and United are Champions League qualification and it was the Red Devils who prevailed 2-1 when the teams met at Old Trafford in September. Roberto De Zerbi made his home debut as Spurs manager against a team he managed for two seasons in his last spell in English football. Plus, Brentford v Fulham, Leeds United v Wolverhampton Wanderers and Newcastle United v Bournemouth

programme.title: The Football Interview
programme.desc: Kelly Somers has a conversation with Nottingham Forest and England midfielder Elliot Anderson

programme.title: Planet of the Apes (2001)
programme.desc: An astronaut crash-lands on a strange planet and is captured and enslaved by the ruling race of highly intelligent apes. Luckily, he is helped by a sympathetic chimpanzee - but then discovers a shocking secret. Remake of the 1960s sci-fi adventure, directed by Tim Burton and starring Mark Wahlberg, Helena Bonham Carter and Tim Roth, and featuring a cameo appearance from the original films star Charlton Heston

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Alistair attends to Paul, who has fallen 20ft off a ladder on to concrete. The specialist paramedic must assess his multiple injuries and reassure his patient, who fears he is dying. Crewmates Isbah and Nando are sent to care for an 83-year-old patient at a doctors surgery, struggling with light-headedness and a high pulse rate of 160bpm, and Jeff and Eddie are dispatched to a patient who has fallen with an injury to her hip

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer visits some of the worlds most dangerous cities to explore the issues that affect young men born into a life on the fringes of society. He begins in the favelas of Rio, Brazil, where he learns what it takes to become a lookout, foot soldier and drug dealer in a world where death could become reality at any moment. He also comes face-to-face with a gun-toting militia boss to uncover how these men rule through fear and intimidation

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Talking Doctor Who
programme.desc: David Tennant time travels back through the BBC archive to tell the story of the Doctors classic era, in a programme originally shown as part of the 60th anniversary celebrations. The selection features rarely seen interviews and clips covering William Hartnells first incarnation right through to the seventh Doctor, Sylvester McCoy, and David compares his experiences with those who came before him

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Dance Race
programme.desc: New Zealand choreographer Corey Bakers short film which a range of dancers, from ballet and Strictly stars to choreographed skydivers and drivers, race across the UK to meet Lady G

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores habitats shared by humans and dangerous creatures, and helps remove a venomous snake from the garden of a family in South America

programme.title: Puss in Boots (2011)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling feline joins forces with a cunning thief and a treacherous old friend to steal a magical golden goose from a giants castle. He must save the day when it transpires that their actions have unleashed a terrible danger on his home town. Animated comedy spin-off from the Shrek movies, with the voices of Antonio Banderas and Salma Hayek

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A famous chef is poisoned at the grand opening of his latest hotel, during a cooking competition in which various hopefuls compete for the head chef position. The team are left confounded when they realise that the victim didnt consume anything that day that all the contestants hadnt also consumed themselves. Catherine finds herself entangled in the case when an old friend becomes a murder suspect

programme.title: Legend (2015)
programme.desc: Biopic of the Kray brothers, exploring their rise to power in the London underworld and conflicts with rival gangs from the point of view of Reggies wife Frances, who remains devoted to her husband even as he remains equally bound to his brutal, unstable sibling. Starring Tom Hardy as both twins, with Emily Browning, Christopher Eccleston and David Thewlis

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is offered a job on a cruise ship but realises taking it will mean leaving Jonny and Corinthian behind. Louise learns the truth about her parents and Donna goes into business

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donnas career falters just as Janets is taking off. Louise gets a letter from her mother but cant face reading it, and proud father Jonny leads the charge in the fight for baby-changing facilities in the pub

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Donna Nobles past catches up with her, as she finds herself reunited with the time traveller. The first of three editions originally broadcast for the shows 60th anniversary, starring David Tennant as the 14th Doctor and Catherine Tate as companion Donna Noble

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An out-of-control Tardis takes the Doctor and Donna to the furthest edge of adventure, to face the most desperate fight of their lives, with the fate of the universe at stake

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The giggle of a mysterious puppet is driving the human race insane. When the Doctor discovers the return of an old enemy, he faces a fight he can never win. Neil Patrick Harris guest stars as 1960s villain the Toymaker and Ncuti Gatwa makes his debut as the 15th Doctor

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short film about four very different schoolgirls thrown together as they plan their escape from detention. Written by and starring Sabrina Ali

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Matt Smith and David Tennant star in 2013s 50th anniversary special, as a mystery at the National Gallery in the present day and an invasion of shape-shifting aliens in Elizabethan times bring two incarnations of the Time Lord together. A greater crisis looms with the arrival of a mysterious third Doctor, who is facing a battle for the fate of the universe and the most terrible decision of his many lives. With Jenna Coleman, and guest starring John Hurt, Billie Piper and Joanna Page

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Robin has an epiphany that has a far-reaching impact on the rest of the ghosts, who each struggle to believe his prediction. Alison suggests a spontaneous night out with Mike, but on trying to get the old gang together find only Obi and his new girlfriend Brenda are available. Despite Alisons best efforts, the evening doesnt quite meet expectations and the couple are left pondering how much their lives are changing

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison receive a life-changing offer beyond their wildest dreams. Still reeling from the news, and anxious to discuss it, they are interrupted by a heartbroken Obi, who has been suddenly dumped by a strangely callous Brenda. Alison and Mike agree to reconvene with lists of pros and cons. At first, Obis romantic woes find a sympathetic ear in eternally lovelorn Thomas, but even he tires of Obis self-pity. Meanwhile Alison must fend off the Ghosts, desperate to sway her decision

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Winnie is ready to hit the road - but first needs to pass her driving test. Naturally, Agnes and the gang volunteer to help her practise, and chaos soon follows. With Winnie behind the wheel and Buster instructing, Finglas becomes a danger zone. Comedy, co-written by and starring Brendan OCarroll, with Danny OCarroll and guest stars Shobu Kapoor and Marek Larwood

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: After accidentally picking up one of Cathys novels, Agnes discovers she has a surprising fondness for steamy fiction. She then gatecrashes the next group meeting of Cathys book group to share her insights on the genre, - mortifying her daughter in the process. Comedy, co-written by and starring Brendan OCarroll, with Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise has a religious experience, while Jonny tries to get Janets attention. Janet finds it difficult to choose a godmother and Gaz starts taking HRT tablets in an effort to get in touch with his feminine side. Comedy, starring Kathryn Drysdale

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny tries to get a job to prove he can be a responsible father - and a position at the biscuit factory might be just the line of work for him. Louise dates her mobile phone before finding a fantastic boyfriend, and Gaz discovers a dream home to share with Donna

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny goes for an interview at the biscuit factory. Janet gets the chance to become a star, and Gaz confronts his fear of sheep head-on, while Louise tries desperately to hang on to Mick. Comedy, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. After a video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise gets a shock when she meets her birth mother for the first time. Gaz decides he has to make a real commitment to Donna, and Janet talks dirty for a living. Starring Kathryn Drysdale and Will Mellor

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Louise meets her dad for the first time, but he shows more interest in Jonny than in her. Donna gives Gaz an answer to his marriage proposal and Janet comes up with a new way of supporting her child - which is not strictly legal

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short film about four very different schoolgirls thrown together as they plan their escape from detention. Written by and starring Sabrina Ali

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the first of Doctor Whos 60th anniversary special episodes

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Matt Smith and David Tennant star in 2013s 50th anniversary special, as a mystery at the National Gallery in the present day and an invasion of shape-shifting aliens in Elizabethan times bring two incarnations of the Time Lord together. A greater crisis looms with the arrival of a mysterious third Doctor, who is facing a battle for the fate of the universe and the most terrible decision of his many lives. With Jenna Coleman, and guest starring John Hurt, Billie Piper and Joanna Page

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre reports on Donald Trumps hardline immigration policies, which have left an estimated 14 million undocumented immigrants are at risk of deportation and resulted in a sharp rise in arrests in US cities and protesters being shot dead on the streets of Minneapolis by federal agents

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: A group hike up Scafell Pike brings buried emotions and romantic confusion to the surface. During an unforeseen storm, Mary finds herself caught in the middle of rising tensions between Mr Hayward and Mr Ryder. As their expedition takes a turn for the worse, Mary is faced with real danger, and the events of the day leave her feeling more conflicted than ever before

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: Charting Michael Jacksons journey from child prodigy to global icon, examining the genius, controversy, trials and tragedy that shaped and continue to divide his legacy. With unique access to friends and family including Jacksons sister, La Toya, this edition charts Michael Jacksons journey from child performer to a deeply troubled musical icon

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, where mother and daughter Liz and Alice compete to raise money for Great Ormond Street Hospital. With just £100 each and 60 minutes on the clock, they scour local charity shops, taking a gamble on vintage fashion, ceramics and the bold purchase of a sewing machine

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has to navigate his way through an eight-bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui Joseph takes in the views in Dover, and Tommy Walsh looks around a bungalow in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. Zoe may be a beginner but the science is on her side - getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: The singer heads back to Ireland to reconnect with family and old friends, explore the dramatic western coastline and experience the countrys unique culture, beginning in the county of Cork He meets up with his nephew Ruairí and together they face off in the ancient sport of road bowling along the back roads of the port town of Castletown Berehaven. Ronan also forages for edible plants in a rewilding project area and visits the countrys first Tibetan Buddhist Temple and Retreat Centre on the Beara Peninsula

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Cubas capital, Clive takes on a potential Olympic champion at one of Havanas legendary boxing gyms. He visits a car mechanic to get under the hood of a classic Chevy and discovers why most cars in Cuba are pre-1959 models. Clive also follows in the footsteps of the Buena Vista Social Club to find out how Cuban rhythms conquered the world

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: In Turkey, Michael Scott uses 3D-scanning technology to examine the once enormous Hippodrome, which began as a stadium for chariot racing, though its foundations were later converted into a huge water cistern to supply the Emperors Great Palace. Michael unearths a holy well, hidden 30 feet under a carpet shop and, conversely, walks high above the city on the most sophisticated and longest aqueduct of the Roman world, finding that part of the Great Palace of Constantine is now a burnt-out shell. Along the way Michael finds time for a shave in the Sultans Grande Bazaar and uncovers the engineering innovations of the Suleymaniye Mosque

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents from Nottingham, where the team discovers a pair of outsize boots whose owner could have weighed 52 stone. Other items include memorabilia from the Nuremberg Trials, a shirt worn by an Arsenal player in 1950 and a neo-classical ring found in the spout of a teapot

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Gloucester Cathedral, presenter Paul Martin, along with experts Catherine Southon and Philip Serrell, views some exceptional items brought in for valuation

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: TV presenter Jacqui Joseph, news broadcaster Ben Thompson, weather forecaster Laura Tobin and comedian Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills before the days winner - and the weeks overall victor - is revealed

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Japanese Alps: Nagano to Narai Juku
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ends the first leg of his journey on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps. In the town of Obuse, Michael learns how Japans most famous artwork, Hokusais The Great Wave off Kanagawa, was made. He visits the largest wasabi farm in the world, and heads to the historic mountain town of Narai Juku, with its beautifully preserved wooden houses

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Patrick Grant joins Diarmuid and Carol as they explore three more contenders for Northern Irelands Greatest Garden. They visit a coastal sanctuary in Millisle, a 15-acre woodland and wildlife garden in Ballymoney, and a country cottage with herbaceous borders and a cut flower garden in Greyabbey

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum Clunie demonstrates what can be grown from just one container in a season, and shows what can be produced for a family of four from a medium-sized vegetable garden. Brian Cunningham looks at how to keep lawns looking great and tackles a patio space around Beechgroves chiminea corner, while there is a visit to a new allotment in Perth

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don previews a new garden in Londons Regents Park to commemorate the life of Queen Elizabeth II, due to open on what would have been her 100th birthday. The team also go behind the scenes of the project, with Advolly Richmond uncovering the parks past, from its origins as a Tudor royal hunting ground to its reinvention as wartime allotments, and Adam Frost helping volunteers plant 11,000 spring bulbs

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Conor Spackman is on the trail of Irish home repair scammers crossing America and ripping off millions in cash. The scam starts with a small repair, then escalates into major work that will supposedly save your home - but its really funding a lavish life for the scammers. With the FBI on their case, Conor Spackman follows the money trail and lifts the lid on where the cash goes

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Phil Wang, Brennan Reece, Rachel Parris
programme.desc: Phil Wang comperes the comedy show at Londons Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by star of The Mash Report Rachel Parris and actor, voiceover artist and stand-up Brennan Reece

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: The Wicker Man (1973)
programme.desc: A devoutly Christian Scottish police officer travels to a remote island to investigate the disappearance of a missing girl. As his investigations are disrupted at every turn, the detective is horrified to see the population have embraced an ancient pagan lifestyle, and begins to fear they may intend to sacrifice the child in an occult ritual. Horror, starring Edward Woodward, Christopher Lee and Britt Ekland.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of a first-round match from day one at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West England competition draws to an end, with the final judging, where the two remaining contestants must prepare dishes celebrating the British film industry. Actor, comedian and Wallace and Gromit voice artist Ben Whtehead joins judges Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, and Phil Wang to decide who will be going through to the final

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary returns to London, where she receives an unexpected offer and faces renewed pressure from Mrs Bennet to find a match. After a run-in with an old friend, and with Mr Hayward absent from London, Mary wonders what happiness could look like for her. Can she find the courage to finally stand up to her mother and forge a path of her own?

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.sub-title: How Pakistan positioned itself as a peace broker
programme.desc: Farhat Javed reports on Pakistans role in the US-Iran ceasefire, and a look at how routine visits for medical treatment in Pakistan have led to children becoming infected with HIV

programme.desc: Cosima and Big Lizard record some cosmic nature sounds for Dad

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of the puma in Patagonia, seeing a tiny cub and witnessing a female hunting an armadillo

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don previews a new garden in Londons Regents Park to commemorate the life of Queen Elizabeth II, due to open on what would have been her 100th birthday. The team also go behind the scenes of the project, with Advolly Richmond uncovering the parks past, from its origins as a Tudor royal hunting ground to its reinvention as wartime allotments, and Adam Frost helping volunteers plant 11,000 spring bulbs

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Live coverage of the morning session on day one at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the start of Zhao Xintong and Mark Allens respective first-round matches against qualifiers. Zhao became the first Chinese winner of this event when he defeated Mark Williams 18-12 in last years final. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: Scotland v England (Kick-off 1.30pm). Coverage of the opening match in the second round of fixtures, held at Scottish Gas Murrayfield. England thrashed Scotland 59-7 in Leicester last year, racing into a 42-0 lead, and they went on to win the Grand Slam, while the Scots finished fourth in the table with two wins and three defeats

programme.title: Elizabeth: The Unseen Queen
programme.desc: The story of Elizabeth II in her own words, featuring home movie footage from her childhood up to her 1953 Coronation at the age of 27. It features a clip marking the first extended visit of Prince Philip to Balmoral in 1946, while the couples engagement was still not public, and depicts Princess Elizabeth as a young mother with Prince Charles and Princess Anne spending time with their grandparents, the king and queen

programme.title: The Championship
programme.desc: Derry v Antrim (Throw-in 5.00pm). Mark Sidebottom presents coverage of the Ulster Senior Football Championship quarter-final at Find Insurance Celtic Park. Derry were defeated by eventual champions Donegal in the preliminary round last year, and Antrim lost to Armagh at this stage. With reports from Sarah Mulkerrins, and commentary by Thomas Niblock and Philly McMahon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, the 17th-century Palladian house owned by the Marquess of Hertford, antiques experts Charlie Ross and Will Axon find great treasures brought by visitors including an interesting busby hat. Will gets a surprise when a Worcester coffee set goes under the hammer, and presenter Paul Martin explores the Battle of Edgehill

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: Steeped in myth and untamed beauty, Dartmoors vast moorlands are characterised by open heathland, grassy hills and striking granite tors, providing a sanctuary for rare plant and animal species, as well as a treasure trove for archaeologists. Rivers meandering through the moors are home to salmon, otters and kingfishers, and peatlands host rare plants like the carnivorous sundew

programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC: Volume 1
programme.desc: Trevor Nelson present a selection of archive performances in a celebration of some of the very best in black British music from over the decades, featuring 40 tracks from artists and genres that have been a vital part of the national soundtrack. The eclectic playlist covers pop, soul, ska, reggae, drum n bass and more, featuring acts including Soul II Soul, Hot Chocolate, Neneh Cherry, Sade, Winifred Atwell, Olivia Dean, So Solid Crew and Stormzy

programme.title: Glastonbury 2019

The Correction Inquiry hears a shocking revelation that puts Garland in the glare of the worlds media. E-Squadron goes on the offensive, and with few allies left to trust, sacrifice is inevitable - just how far is Rachel Carey willing to go to protect the truth?

programme.desc: Throughout the world there are mysterious ancient stones shaped into perfect spheres. Many of them show clear evidence of being manmade and some completely defy explanation. The mystery of the stone spheres could provide clues about humankinds past

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: A fire-breathing crater burns in the middle of a desert - and some suspect it is a gateway to Hell. Scientists try to work out how an eight-foot-long Bull Shark ended up in a golf course water feature. They want to know if a sharknado event lifted the creature out of the sea and dumped it inland. A photographer captures an ominous ring hovering in the sky over downtown Chicago

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind the Deal
programme.desc: A second look at some of the greatest Hardcore Pawn episodes. Les is back on top, taking full control of the store, but Seth and Ashley think he might be pushing too far, too fast

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind the Deal
programme.desc: Some of the greatest Hardcore Pawn episodes, as seen through the eyes of the Golds

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn: Behind the Deal
programme.desc: Les, Seth, and Ashley remember Tressa, a former employee and Ashleys nemesis, and her scheme to scam American Jewellery

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques dealers Debbie Serpell, Helen Gardiner, Stewart Hofgartner and Tim Hogarth visit Carlisle. Assortments of needlework, jewellery, silver and porcelain are among the items brought in to be valued by members of the public, who then decide whether to put their treasures up for auction or accept a cash sum

programme.title: Wicked Tuna
programme.desc: Dave Carraros feud with Tyler McLaughlin intensifies and he becomes determined to teach his rival a lesson. TJ Ott and Dave Marciano put their inexperienced new crew to the test

programme.title: Wicked Tuna
programme.desc: A series of bad decisions could spell doom for Pinwheel as they continue to fight FV-Tuna.com at the top of the leader board

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Pickle must face her fear of big cats as she continues to impress Troy with her natural snake hunting talent. Bill and Tes stumble on a rumoured government testing facility and find all they can handle

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Troy and Pickle attempt to track down a giant python known as the Night Serpent. Meanwhile, Tess convinces Bill to try out new hunting grounds

programme.title: Gold Wars Downunder
programme.desc: Three hardcore mining teams battle it out on the one patch of gold-rich-land to see who can unearth the biggest nuggets and the most gold. Socialite couple Kimberley and Ritchie White make the tough decision to move from the Gold Coast to the mine to help out

programme.title: Gold Wars Downunder
programme.desc: Three hardcore mining teams battle it out on the one patch of gold-rich-land at the town of Clermont to see who can unearth the biggest nuggets and the most gold. There are nine weeks left of the gold season to go and the pressure is on. At Swiss Diggings, an unemployed Donnie begs owner Dennis for a new job

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Ten mysterious mountains that are notorious for otherworldly phenomena. They include a summit in Sri Lanka that is said to be a portal to other worlds and a volcano in Washington that was at the heart of a UFO sighting

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.desc: Tony Harris separates the real from the fake in some of the most compelling Bigfoot evidence recently captured. Footprint casts from Mount St Helens may be proof that a massive, undiscovered primate lives in North America

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Caddy the Cadborosaurus, the Lubbock Lights and Living Mummy
programme.desc: How a fishermans footage may hold the key to unravelling the mystery of a giant sea serpent known as Caddy the Cadborosaurus. Plus, the enigmatic case of the Lubbock Lights, where hundreds of people witnessed strange blue and green flashing lights illuminating the Texas skies, and the story of a centenarian monk attempting to mummify himself

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman and Ancient Horn Mystery
programme.desc: The legendary Ohio Grassman is apparently captured in footage of a tall, dark-coloured, hairy creature walking through woods. Plus, a look at the Chilean family photograph of a real UFO flying above the Andes Mountains and the notion that a 5,000-year-old horn discovered on a beach in England belongs to a prehistoric creature that still lives

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Satellites orbiting the planet more eyes on Earths vast, unexplored areas of land and sea - revealing baffling new anomalies

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: The people, places and objects plagued by what can only be described as a curse. This episode considers a diamond with a sinister past, the bones of a world-famous bard, and a beach covered in cursed stones

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: Timur was the last Central Asian conqueror to achieve great military successes as a leader of nomad warrior lords, ruling both agricultural and pastoral peoples on an imperial scale. A rumour abounds that the tomb of the Turco-Mongol conqueror is cursed such that whoever disturbs it will face a calamity

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: How showman, politician and businessman PT Barnum made millions off the backs of the defenseless while, one by one, his properties went up in flames. Plus, the string of eager prospectors who tried their luck in uncharted territory, and the 200 men and women who sacrificed themselves to a massive pyre

programme.title: Holy Marvels with Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: From the legendary blades of ancient kings to a divine arsenal said to annihilate monsters, history is filled with tales of iconic sacred weapons. In this episode, the actor delves into how they were crafted, who dared to wield them, and the epic battles that forged their place in myth

programme.title: Field of Vampires
programme.desc: Part one of two. A plot in Poland containing 30 or so apparent deviant burials. One woman was buried with a sickle across her neck and a padlock on her toe - this corpse was believed to be a real-life vampire. An international team of experts uses cutting-edge science and historical research to investigate who this woman was and why she was so feared

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Cameras capture something shocking in a cemetery: empty graves, as if the dead walked out in the middle of the night; using cutting-edge tech, experts investigate whats behind this apparent evidence of zombie activity

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: Unexplained phenomena occurring across North Americas National Parks. This episode focuses on Big Bend National Park in Texas, where a series of incidents has left some experts convinced of secret alien activities taking place there

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: Unexplained phenomena occurring across North Americas National Parks. This episode explores the apparent forces living in the secret underworld of Death Valley National Park on the California-Nevada border, where a series of deaths and disappearances have taken place

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Investigating whether aliens could be plotting an attack on Earth, and if humans could survive their weapons terrifying capabilities

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: In the spring of 1952, reports of UFO sightings rose significantly. Former US Air Officials attempt to shed light on the possible causes

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: In 1969, the US government officially announced it would stop investigating UFOs. However, declassified documents reveal that multiple government agencies have been hiding important UFO sightings for decades

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Considering why the space race stopped at the moon and whether there any truth to rumours of a secret Space Force developed by America for missions beyond Earths orbit

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: A look at some dangerous fighter pilot encounters with UFOs, featuring the Thomas F Mantell Incident where a UFO is reported to have shot down a military plane killing its pilot. Plus, the Sao Paulo UFO Incident, the Tehran Incidents and a further case in Peru where a military aircraft shot a missile at a UFO and scored a hit

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: For decades, governments around the world have investigated UFO sightings. This programme considers how such top-secret studies are conducted

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos from Ancient Aliens investigates whether extra-terrestrial beings lie at the heart of some of Earths most famous mysteries

programme.title: TFI Friday Unplugged
programme.desc: Chris Evans brings his entertainment show back to Channel 4 in a more intimate format, featuring interviews with the biggest stars and music by the latest bands from the presenters studio at Virgin Radio UK. Tonight he is joined by Sam Ryder, Peter Capaldi and Gemma Arterton

programme.title: Sky Coppers
programme.desc: The team join the search for a woman who has left a suicide note and vanished into the night. And officers are shocked when they arrive at a house to discover a gang cruelly breeding dangerous dogs in horrific conditions

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Critically ill dialysis patient Gillian desperately needs fluids, so the East Anglian Air Ambulance crew must make a tough decision. In Canvey Island, the medics take over the breathing of motorcyclist Sol who is bleeding from his ear. In Luton, Essex and Herts Air Ambulance help 16-year-old Idris who has a head and leg injury after a collision with a car. In Norwich, Martin has fallen from a ladder and has a nasty open ankle fracture

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including the Mixing Bowl, the Olde Stone Mill, Peters and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: Alison Hammond presents the show giving people the chance to perform the one song that tells their story - often in front of a crowd for the very first time. Mentors Paloma Faith and Sam Ryder watch and listen to the performances. In the first episode, Alison heads to Liverpool, where a teenager sings Bocelli for the foster mum he cant find words to thank, and a kidney transplant recipient brings Sam and Paloma sprinting out into the downpour. With only one place available at the grand final at Londons Hackney Empire, Sam and Paloma face an impossible choice

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets Andi and Neil from York, who dream of retiring permanently to Spain with their son Joel and spaniel Dixie. Theyre looking for a three-bedroom house with a dog-friendly garden, where barbeque enthusiast Neil can cook up a storm close to the beach close to the coastal town of Mazarron, near Murcia. Laura has five sizzling options for their budget of £145,000, but will any of them turn out to be their new forever home?

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke helps unlock the potential of two houses in south London. He begins by helping NHS theatre nurse Hannah, whose Edwardian workers cottage in Thornton Heath is so small she has had to give the only decent bedroom to her two children while she sleeps in the front room. Can he provide the luxurious master bedroom she needs? George also helps a couple renovate their Streatham home, which would not be so bad if not for the previous owners love of glass chandeliers, Roman columns and marble-effect tiles

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam invents an elaborate lie to explain to his friends the humiliating injury he suffered in an encounter with a jealous husband. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Coach falls for an unscrupulous woman who drops him when she wins the lottery, while Sam lusts after her daughter. Bette Ford and Ellen Regan guest star

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam fails to make an impression on the object of his desire, and Coach faces a tough test when the woman he loves dumps him after a big-money win on the lottery

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: All Ray wants for Christmas is a little loving from his wife, but their hopes for an intimate Christmas are ruined by their familys constant interruptions

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Enlisting for an American Civil War re-enactment allows Ray to spend time with his father, until he realises they will be fighting on opposite sides. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ally has to interview her oldest living relative as part of a school project on genealogy, so Ray helps her track down Franks long-lost Aunt Sarina, who lives in Italy but turns up on the Barones doorstep

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Crane brothers confront an unscrupulous car-park attendant over a matter of principle - and two dollars. Meanwhile, Daphne leaves Frasiers home for the last time, and realises how much Eddie and Martin are going to miss her. Kelsey Grammer stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink hopes to become the new condominium president and tries to ensure he is elected by persuading an unsuspecting Martin to stand as a puppet candidate. American comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Rozs fun-loving 20-year-old cousin visits Seattle, and Frasier finds himself struggling to keep pace with her partying. Meanwhile, Martin has trouble coping without Daphne. Long-running American comedy, guest starring Zooey Deschanel (New Girl)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Bulldog plays a practical joke on Frasier, who wastes no time in plotting revenge. Meanwhile, Niles and Daphne get caught up in a battle with a doorstep trick-or-treater. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the concluding part of his trip to the Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, Gordons disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet. Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Staffordshire to meet petrolhead Martin, whos also the organiser of a local music festival

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers are greeted by the Duchess of Northumberland on a visit to Alnwick Castle, where Matt is granted special access to the records tower, which holds precious documents detailing its role in major historical events. Janice follows in the footsteps of the castles archers of old, trying her hand with a bow and arrow in the grounds, and the family meet the Dukes official piper for the ultimate lesson on the ancient Northumbrian pipes

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps best friends of 50 years Jan and Chris find their dream holiday home in Almería, Spain, that works for both their families. In fact, between them they have six children and five grandchildren - so it could be a challenge to please everyone. With a joint budget of £150,000, Laura lines up five great options, but will Jan and Chris fall in love with one enough to end up making an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In northern Italy, ambitious pair Kevin and Laura battle against the odds to finish their tricky renovations in time for the arrival of their first retreat guests. In Spain, cameras are back with BandB owners Kirsty and Simon as they try to extend their season. Meanwhile, new parents Vicky and Aurelian rise to the challenge as they open a bakery in France

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In the Loire Valley at Château La Grande Maison, Ben and Billies unfinished apartment gets their hearts racing as try to complete it for a decadent Valentines Day event. At the Château Trois Cloches, Chris and Jolyon are tackling a full façade transformation. Near Bordeaux at Château Meaume, Mark and Sandra juggle a full gîte renovation with managing a vineyard and tractor lessons

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Japan is often portrayed as a modern utopia, but beneath its polished exterior, one of the worlds oldest populations is reaching a breaking point. programme.title: Commando (1985)
programme.desc: The daughter of retired special forces officer John Matrix is kidnapped by an old comrade-in-arms, now in league with a deposed former dictator. The tyrant plans to use the girl to force Matrix into assassinating the head of the country he once ruled, but is unprepared for the lengths to which the veteran soldier will go to rescue his child. Action adventure, with Arnold Schwarzenegger and Rae Dawn Chong

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: The King of Queens
