programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel tries to fix a cocker spaniels leg problems by fitting two bionic back feet, before launching a series of investigations into what is causing a cat to drag its leg. Plus, a Westie puppy with a fractured knee arrives, but it cannot keep still

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Pioneering veterinarian Noel Fitzpatrick plans to use groundbreaking cell technology to repair damaged cartilage in a 10-month-old golden retriever that helps a five-year-old girl cope with epilepsy. A cat arrives at the surgery with a suspected rat bite that seems to be getting bigger, and a whippet is brought in after being hit by a car - sustaining multiple pelvic fractures

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, with a guest appearance by Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Ex-EastEnders star Jill Halfpenny and sitcom actor Brian Murphy guest star in the sketch show, featuring Catherine Tate as an assortment of bizarre characters

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy accompanies Nessa to the hospital for her first ultrasound scan, and the pair catch a glimpse of their baby. Stacey becomes unusually excited at the prospect of a weekend visit to her family home after she struggles to adjust to her role as an Essex wife - which proves to lack some of the glamour she had expected

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team of experts make a property in Cork wheelchair-friendly for Adam Drummond. The 22-year-old former Irish International basketball player is currently adjusting to life in a wheelchair, and the team sets out to adapt his parents house in order to allow Adam to live in his family home once again

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Dominic Harrison - aka singer and musician Yungblud - as he joins him during his whirlwind tour. Along the way, Louis meets fans who follow Yungblud with almost religious intensity, before sitting down with the singer to discuss a range of topics including his mental health, his sexuality and his response to outspoken critics

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to a 67-year-old with a lung condition who cant breathe. Plus, crews help a 53-year-old whos had several seizures

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sally and Millie are called to a domestic affray in which a man throws out his ex-heroin addict partner after finding drugs on the doormat. Despite her protests of innocence, she ends up on the street and is soon pulled in for shoplifting, only to learn her five-year-old daughter has gone missing. CCTV footage reveals the culprits but also draws attention to a homeless man (guest star Oscar James) in the park who seems to know something about the case

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Callum arrives at a crime scene expecting to arrest a woman accused of theft, but the suspect turns out to be a victim of domestic abuse who desperately needs help. Anxious to protect her, he is frustrated by her reluctance to bring charges against her husband and further thwarted when CID identify the man as an armed robber and put him under surveillance, leaving the woman vulnerable to his violent outbursts

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the first of six episodes about the problems of inner-city schools, a reluctant Nate is appointed as Safer Schools Officer at Deansgate Comprehensive following an arson attack on the art department. On his first day he breaks up a fight between two girls who clearly know who is responsible for the fire and Smithy is stabbed in the chest as he pursues the investigation. Convinced there is a job to be done at the school, and hoping to renew his acquaintance with attractive teacher Becky James, Nate volunteers to carry on in the liaison role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a Deansgate Comprehensive pupil collapses in the corridor, Safer Schools Officer Nate finds he has a drug problem on his hands. Trusted pupil Jimmy Ryan is caught in possession of a stash of Ecstasy forced on him by his older brother, who lives in fear of the local dealer, and when Jimmy is taken into care, accusing Nate of breaking his promises, the liaison officer realises what he has let himself in for

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenagers house is trashed in revenge for an alleged rape and Safer Schools Officer Nate is horrified to discover the extent of peer pressure, drinking, underage sex and bullying among the pupils of Deansgate Comprehensive. Allegations of rape at a booze-fuelled teenage party leave him wondering whether he will ever be able to make his mark in his new role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A hate campaign sparked by a rape case from 18 months previously divides pupils and parents at Deansgate Comprehensive, leading to schoolyard confrontations and classroom fights. Safer Schools Officer Nate discovers the victim of the slanderous campaign is entirely innocent of the crime, and the identity of the person spreading malicious lies comes as a terrible shock

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A break-in at Deansgate Comprehensive proves to be little more than a prank by two youngsters, but a school governor takes his daughter away, blaming the staff for her atypical behaviour when she confesses her part in the crime. Teacher Becky James fails to turn up for work and Nate finds her beaten unconscious at her home by someone who desperately wants to get hold of her laptop

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A skier falls over on a steep slope and suffers a suspected brain injury in Wakefield and a DIY enthusiasts attempts to fix the heating in his chilly home end in disaster

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads to a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: The Auckland Rescue Helicopter crew are dispatched to a critical situation when a drag racer smashes into a concrete wall

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: Four people are left fighting to keep their heads above water when their boat sinks in the Firth of Thames

programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. programme.desc: Nick Knowles hits the road to catch up with some of the inspirational people and communities the show has helped in its 20-year history. On his travels across the length and breadth of the country, Nick returns to some of the series biggest and most challenging builds, including Canada Street in Manchester, The Yard in Edinburgh, and DIY SOSs most logistically-difficult and longest build - the construction of the Grenfell Community Centre and Dale Youth Boxing club

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert dons his hard hat as guest presenter for this ambitious build in Brandesburton near Hull, East Yorkshire. The team are joined by local volunteers to transform an overgrown 2.5 acres into a purpose-built adventure campsite for the St Michaels Youth Project (St Mikes), which receives funding from Children in Need

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth are kept busy with a man vomiting blood and a patients hip pain

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The newlyweds return from their honeymoon to a big welcome from both their families. However, Smithy seems disconcerted by his pals return and Nessa makes a confession that spoils the happy occasion. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne, James Corden and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: The comediennes bizarre characters run riot as mouthy schoolgirl Lauren visits the gym, wig-makers to the stars Ivan and Trudy go about their business and DI Angie Barker demonstrates how hard it is to be a tough cop and a mother. Una Stubbs guest stars

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team of experts make a property in Cork wheelchair-friendly for Adam Drummond. The 22-year-old former Irish International basketball player is currently adjusting to life in a wheelchair, and the team sets out to adapt his parents house in order to allow Adam to live in his family home once again

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Dominic Harrison - aka singer and musician Yungblud - as he joins him during his whirlwind tour. Along the way, Louis meets fans who follow Yungblud with almost religious intensity, before sitting down with the singer to discuss a range of topics including his mental health, his sexuality and his response to outspoken critics

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to a 67-year-old with a lung condition who cant breathe. Plus, crews help a 53-year-old whos had several seizures

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sally and Millie are called to a domestic affray in which a man throws out his ex-heroin addict partner after finding drugs on the doormat. Despite her protests of innocence, she ends up on the street and is soon pulled in for shoplifting, only to learn her five-year-old daughter has gone missing. CCTV footage reveals the culprits but also draws attention to a homeless man (guest star Oscar James) in the park who seems to know something about the case

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Callum arrives at a crime scene expecting to arrest a woman accused of theft, but the suspect turns out to be a victim of domestic abuse who desperately needs help. Anxious to protect her, he is frustrated by her reluctance to bring charges against her husband and further thwarted when CID identify the man as an armed robber and put him under surveillance, leaving the woman vulnerable to his violent outbursts

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the first of six episodes about the problems of inner-city schools, a reluctant Nate is appointed as Safer Schools Officer at Deansgate Comprehensive following an arson attack on the art department. On his first day he breaks up a fight between two girls who clearly know who is responsible for the fire and Smithy is stabbed in the chest as he pursues the investigation. Convinced there is a job to be done at the school, and hoping to renew his acquaintance with attractive teacher Becky James, Nate volunteers to carry on in the liaison role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a Deansgate Comprehensive pupil collapses in the corridor, Safer Schools Officer Nate finds he has a drug problem on his hands. Trusted pupil Jimmy Ryan is caught in possession of a stash of Ecstasy forced on him by his older brother, who lives in fear of the local dealer, and when Jimmy is taken into care, accusing Nate of breaking his promises, the liaison officer realises what he has let himself in for

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenagers house is trashed in revenge for an alleged rape and Safer Schools Officer Nate is horrified to discover the extent of peer pressure, drinking, underage sex and bullying among the pupils of Deansgate Comprehensive. Allegations of rape at a booze-fuelled teenage party leave him wondering whether he will ever be able to make his mark in his new role

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A hate campaign sparked by a rape case from 18 months previously divides pupils and parents at Deansgate Comprehensive, leading to schoolyard confrontations and classroom fights. Safer Schools Officer Nate discovers the victim of the slanderous campaign is entirely innocent of the crime, and the identity of the person spreading malicious lies comes as a terrible shock

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A break-in at Deansgate Comprehensive proves to be little more than a prank by two youngsters, but a school governor takes his daughter away, blaming the staff for her atypical behaviour when she confesses her part in the crime. Teacher Becky James fails to turn up for work and Nate finds her beaten unconscious at her home by someone who desperately wants to get hold of her laptop

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A skier falls over on a steep slope and suffers a suspected brain injury in Wakefield and a DIY enthusiasts attempts to fix the heating in his chilly home end in disaster

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads to a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: The Auckland Rescue Helicopter crew are dispatched to a critical situation when a drag racer smashes into a concrete wall

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: Four people are left fighting to keep their heads above water when their boat sinks in the Firth of Thames

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: The newlyweds return from their honeymoon to a big welcome from both their families. However, Smithy seems disconcerted by his pals return and Nessa makes a confession that spoils the happy occasion. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne, James Corden and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessas shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy accompanies Nessa to the hospital for her first ultrasound scan, and the pair catch a glimpse of their baby. Stacey becomes unusually excited at the prospect of a weekend visit to her family home after she struggles to adjust to her role as an Essex wife - which proves to lack some of the glamour she had expected

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Award-winning sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and oddball characters, with a guest appearance by Siobhan Redmond

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Ex-EastEnders star Jill Halfpenny and sitcom actor Brian Murphy guest star in the sketch show, featuring Catherine Tate as an assortment of bizarre characters

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews deal with a cycling accident and a woman having a suspected heart attack, while paramedics go above and beyond to get a 24-year-old with allergies the medication she need

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Bigger Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz and the team come to the aid of former firefighter Pa Berry, who suffered extensive injuries in the line of duty and is in need of help in completing his home renovation

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team send their Paul Smith Mini in for a bit of designer tailoring by the team at Mini Sport

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek makes the short trip to a mysterious Yorkshire beauty spot to pick up a 1920s style bus that isnt all that it seems

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcocks films and Graham Greenes books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels though Cumbria, setting sail in a small craft on Lake Windermere with a lifelong devotee of Arthur Ransomes childrens books Swallows and Amazons. At Barrow-in-Furness, Michael investigates the mountains of scrap left behind as iron ore was turned into ships, trains and railway tracks, and discovers researchers are exploring how slagbanks could contribute to reducing atmospheric carbon dioxide.

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the last leg of his railway journey around the north-west coast of England, visiting the childhood home of William Wordsworth in Cockermouth, Cumbria, and ending in Maryport, where the Solway Firth marks the border between England and Scotland

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins the hunt for dark matter in the deepest mine in Britain in Boulby, North Yorkshire, then visits Middlesbroughs Transporter Bridge, which offers high views over the Tees estuary and the Cleveland Hills. At Saltholme, Michael joins the RSPB in a hide to observe a visiting bird of prey

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Whitby, where he explores the ports shipbuilding and seafaring heritage, to Robin Hoods Bay, a former smuggling haven

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks off to Staffordshire to collect an unusual haul, including a Blue Star truck and a mysterious Lion Omnibus Reliant

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A sun-bleached red Vauxhall Astra GTE gives the team a headache. They also buy a classic French moped and catch up on the restoration of a Mini Cooper S that was found in a barn

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchills plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Swanage Pier in Dorset where highlights include a famous pantomime dames costumes, a battered watch with a big price tag and valuable Star Wars memorabilia. Will Farmer challenges Fiona to a guessing game with some of his own Poole Pottery collection that doesnt go entirely to plan

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crystal Palace Park in south-east London, where she is joined by cartoonist Ralph Steadman. Items brought in to be valued include a trio of bronze Chinese censers, a rare sundial by Thomas Tuttell from the 17th century, and a rare do it yourself kit dress by fashion designer Paco Rabanne. Mark Smith challenges Fiona to identify secret Second World War spy gadgets concealed in everyday items

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek points the team towards one of his favourite motors - a 1983 Daimler Double Six. And a Triumph 2000 is given a modern day makeover under the bonnet

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team send their Paul Smith Mini in for a bit of designer tailoring by the team at Mini Sport

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchills plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Swanage Pier in Dorset where highlights include a famous pantomime dames costumes, a battered watch with a big price tag and valuable Star Wars memorabilia. Will Farmer challenges Fiona to a guessing game with some of his own Poole Pottery collection that doesnt go entirely to plan

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of Simons journey takes him from the banks of the Nile to the edge of the Indian Ocean in Oman. Along the way he is introduced to Nubians displaced by the formation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s and nomadic Bedouins who have been forced to move by a seven-year drought scientists believe is due to climate change. On Egypts southern coast, he dives in pristine coral reefs, before seeing the unexpected sides of Saudi Arabian life

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher takes on the role of agony aunt and ends up writing letters home for his fellow inmates - but he soon discovers domestic problems of his own when his daughter brings bad news on visiting day. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Patricia Brake

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber is feeling dejected after his romantic fortunes take a turn for the worse, so Fletch tries to revive his friends flagging spirits. However, his cunning plan has one potential drawback - it involves his own daughter. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek points the team towards one of his favourite motors - a 1983 Daimler Double Six. And a Triumph 2000 is given a modern day makeover under the bonnet

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: In Shropshire a Great Western Railway Modified Hall steam engine and its tender is being taken out of service as it needs a safety inspection

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul is off to the Lake District for a poignant collection from a Mathewsons regular - with seven vehicles to load including a Thames Truck, a Zodiac and a Vauxhall Victor

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien discover items including stained glass and a Victorian hay rack that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the North Yorkshire seaside resort of Scarborough to explore one of the greatest royal fortresses in England

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey in the East Yorkshire resort of Hornsea, famous for its distinctive pottery. In Hull, he discovers how one of Hull FCs star players made history as Britains first black rugby captain. From Hull, Michael hitches a ride on a fine-looking barge heading to Goole on a network of canals. At Spurn Point, Michael hears how along this coast an ingenious 19th-century RNLI invention saved many lives

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo has reached Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey from Middlesbrough ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the towns history - as a maintenance base for some of the largest wind farms in the world. In neighbouring Cleethorpes, Michael rides on the Cleethorpes Coast Light Railway, pulled by a miniature steam locomotive. Later, Michael makes for a curious and wild stretch of coastline at Humberston

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Northern Irelands spectacular Causeway coast to begin a new journey, which takes him along the north and east coasts through Ulster and Leinster. He begins in Co Derry on the windswept shores of Loch Foyle, where the largest accumulation of coastal sands in Ireland has formed a system of dunes visible from space. He also learns how scientists are monitoring the site and hears how their work has been picked up by fellow scientists in the United States to help research the surface of Mars

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Peter wants a Triumph Herald Convertible, like the one he had when he first learned to drive. But now rare, will £9000 be enough to find a good one?

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Rupert has decided life is too short not to follow his dream of owning a Morris Traveller. Will Ruperts £13,000 budget stretch to a good one?

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Eric loves open-top motoring, but his sports car is too low and he needs an automatic car, so Sheldon and Peter suggest a VW Golf Cabriolet that might stretch Erics budget

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Norman has dreamt of owning a Volvo 850R since watching Volvo British touring cars race, but fixers Sheldon and Peter could struggle to bring Normans dream to life

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher takes on the role of agony aunt and ends up writing letters home for his fellow inmates - but he soon discovers domestic problems of his own when his daughter brings bad news on visiting day. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Patricia Brake

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The disappearance of an illicit tin of pineapple chunks leaves the inmates horrified that there is a thief in their midst - and for a while, no one is above suspicion. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber is feeling dejected after his romantic fortunes take a turn for the worse, so Fletch tries to revive his friends flagging spirits. However, his cunning plan has one potential drawback - it involves his own daughter. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britains inland waterways

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of Simons journey takes him from the banks of the Nile to the edge of the Indian Ocean in Oman. Along the way he is introduced to Nubians displaced by the formation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s and nomadic Bedouins who have been forced to move by a seven-year drought scientists believe is due to climate change. On Egypts southern coast, he dives in pristine coral reefs, before seeing the unexpected sides of Saudi Arabian life

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The last leg of Simons journey through South America takes him from Chiles Atacama Desert right down to Tierra Del Fuego, the most southerly inhabited place on Earth. In the Atacama, one of the driest places on the planet with is as little as 1mm of rain per year, Simon discovers shocking evidence of human pollution

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the documentary exploring Earths natural history and the factors that have affected it. The first edition examines how the sun affects life on Earth through the seasons, following a polar bear preparing its cubs for a long journey to avoid becoming stranded on floating glaciers. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A tour of the mightiest rivers in the world, from the deep waters of Siberias Lake Baikal, home to the worlds only freshwater seal, to the wetlands downstream of Earths tallest waterfall patrolled by frenzied piranhas. There is also footage of the lightning-quick ambush of wildebeest by crocodiles in Africa and macaque monkeys swimming underwater to catch crabs in Indonesia. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul collects a cherished 1924 Rover 9 thats been in the same family since the 1950s and only had one previous owner before that

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks drawn the short straw again as he attempts to shift a couple of 1930s Austin Sevens that have been stuck in the same spot for half a century

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century, and the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine thats seen better days

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rusty 1970 Fiat 500 goes under the hammer - Derek Mathewson reckons its the kind of vehicle that is so iconic that it has to be saved

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle thats ripe for restoration

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey in the East Yorkshire resort of Hornsea, famous for its distinctive pottery. In Hull, he discovers how one of Hull FCs star players made history as Britains first black rugby captain. From Hull, Michael hitches a ride on a fine-looking barge heading to Goole on a network of canals. At Spurn Point, Michael hears how along this coast an ingenious 19th-century RNLI invention saved many lives

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo has reached Lincolnshire, where his coastal railway journey from Middlesbrough ends. In Grimsby Town, he investigates a new stage in the towns history - as a maintenance base for some of the largest wind farms in the world. In neighbouring Cleethorpes, Michael rides on the Cleethorpes Coast Light Railway, pulled by a miniature steam locomotive. Later, Michael makes for a curious and wild stretch of coastline at Humberston

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Northern Irelands spectacular Causeway coast to begin a new journey, which takes him along the north and east coasts through Ulster and Leinster. He begins in Co Derry on the windswept shores of Loch Foyle, where the largest accumulation of coastal sands in Ireland has formed a system of dunes visible from space. He also learns how scientists are monitoring the site and hears how their work has been picked up by fellow scientists in the United States to help research the surface of Mars

He then cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming. As he navigates a Roman waterway, it proves to be a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of Simons journey takes him from the banks of the Nile to the edge of the Indian Ocean in Oman. Along the way he is introduced to Nubians displaced by the formation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s and nomadic Bedouins who have been forced to move by a seven-year drought scientists believe is due to climate change. On Egypts southern coast, he dives in pristine coral reefs, before seeing the unexpected sides of Saudi Arabian life

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The last leg of Simons journey through South America takes him from Chiles Atacama Desert right down to Tierra Del Fuego, the most southerly inhabited place on Earth. In the Atacama, one of the driest places on the planet with is as little as 1mm of rain per year, Simon discovers shocking evidence of human pollution

programme.title: Planet Earth

Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A tour of the mightiest rivers in the world, from the deep waters of Siberias Lake Baikal, home to the worlds only freshwater seal, to the wetlands downstream of Earths tallest waterfall patrolled by frenzied piranhas. There is also footage of the lightning-quick ambush of wildebeest by crocodiles in Africa and macaque monkeys swimming underwater to catch crabs in Indonesia. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie help six more people lose weight with different methods. Natasha and Jodian have just two weeks to slim down before a trip to Egypt, with one trying out a high-fat diet and the other eating Mediterranean food. In Hampshire, Mike tries out a regimen that consists almost entirely of specially-made cookies and Steve is on a volumetric diet consisting of large bowls of soup or massive salads, while Gina and Max in Wigan go on paleolithic and starch diets respectively. At the Royal, a painfully thin girl causes concern for Goodwin - whose green-eyed monster emerges when student nurse Samantha Beaumont agrees to a date with a younger man - and the hospital is evacuated after a suicide victims corpse is brought in emitting toxic fumes. programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the worlds fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour

A coveted space in the semi-finals is at stake and with it the chance of a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the Royal Variety Performance. Presented by Ant and Dec

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor Phil Dunster, who rose to fame in hit show Ted Lasso, to talk about his new comedy Rooster. Actress Cush Jumbo chats about the second season of crime thriller Criminal Record, and comedian Josh Widdicombe discusses his podcast The Museum of Pop Culture, while Dan Levy, creator, writer and star of the multi-award winning show Schitts Creek, discusses the series

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Robbie Savage and his son Charlie, Helen Flanagan and her mum Julia, and Tayce and her dad Qariem Radix compete in the comedy game show. The parents choose which questions their children will answer and gamble on how many they can get right. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi travels to the French Pyrenees, where ex-pats Mike and Joss invested everything they had into creating a holiday retreat and a beautiful family home for themselves and their two young boys. But despite the fabulous setting, the hotels bookings are poor and with their savings dwindling, the couple are desperate not to take another wrong turn. Can Alex get them back on the right track?

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Telly fanatics from around the country watch, dissect and offer plenty of opinions about the best telly moments from the past week

programme.title: Cracker
programme.desc: Part one of three. Police psychologist Fitz comes face to face with the dark underbelly of suburbia while investigating the disappearance of a teenage girl mixed up in a bizarre religious cult. Crime drama, starring Robbie Coltrane, Jim Carter and Geraldine Somerville

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Time Is Money
programme.desc: Sara Davies hosts the quiz in which contestants are given their prize money before they have answered a question, and must race against time to keep hold of it. Each player stands before an hourglass-shaped money timer that begins to drain as the questions are asked - and the only way of stopping it is to answer correctly. Once the money is gone, the contestant is out - and only one can make the final to play for the amount they have managed to save

programme.title: Help Me Buy a Home

programme.desc: Exploring the hotel attack that killed David Byrne, ignited a deadly gang war, and the policing and trials that reshaped Irelands crime landscape

programme.title: Danny Dyer: How to Be a Man
programme.desc: Danny Dyer travels the country exploring the contentious subject of modern masculinity, questioning whether men have lost their sense of identity and what it means to be male in 21st-century Britain. Along the way, he tries to get to the bottom of toxic masculinity and find out why so many young men are struggling with their mental health and relationships with women. Danny sets out to find out whether theres a rise in toxic masculinity in society and, if there is, why? He meets up with his brother Tony, before tackling controversial influencer Ed Matthews, meeting teenage boys at a London school, and speaking with Mansfield MP Ben Bradley, whos a champion of mens issues. He also meets male victims of domestic abuse, and in Brighton, he chats to members of the Brighton Gay Mens Chorus about overcoming prejudice

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi is called to the Gipsy Hill Hotel in Exeter, where owner Grace has plans to retire and hand the business to her daughter Jackie, who currently manages the hotel. But profits are falling and Grace worries there will be no business to pass down. Alex immediately spots that the hotel is stuck in the past, with its old-fashioned decor. Can she convince the owner to release her grip on the purse strings and allow her daughter to take ownership now?

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the worlds fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets an 11-year-old meningitis survivor hoping to finally have hands, and a young couple with fertility complications who dream of starting a family. Also taking part are parents whose only wish is for their young son to take his first steps, and a man who, having shed half his body weight, is left with an unsightly physical reminder of his former self

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: The reception team face a change of pace as the holiday season ends. But even if things are quiet, Miguel still has his hands full with maintenance work. Mario has a mystery to solve before some VIP guests check in, while a new entertainer finds himself performing on the graveyard day shift, and sets out to prove his worth by taking charge of a bingo game

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week.

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare dishes for family feasts, starting with roast chicken stuffed with orzo and served on a bed of new potatoes and butterbeans, as well as a warming sausage meatballs and dumplings in a cider sauce. For dessert, its a decadent hazelnut brownie with chocolate sauce and toffee popcorn

programme.title: Alex Polizzis Secret Italy
programme.desc: Alex embraces her lifelong love of Italy and travels North to South, taking in the breath-taking cities of Venice, Milan, Rome and the Amalfi Coast, before unearthing hidden tourist gems in Puglia. She begins in Sardinia

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show which takes place in public locations around the UK. He invites passers-by in Glasgow to take part in a series of unusual challenges, with a prize of £1,000 up for grabs

programme.desc: Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in SOS: Survive or Surrender, hosted by former commando Jason Fox, who is in on the whole thing. Actor and comedian Guz Khan is the guest saboteur

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where six hopefuls compete for £1 million. However, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: Ireland v Italy (Kick-off 5.40pm). Coverage of both teams second match of the Championship, held at Dexcom Stadium. Ireland claimed a dominant 54-212 away win when the teams met in last years competition and finished two places above their opponents in the table in third, but Le Azzurre won 27-21 on Irish soil in 2024

programme.title: Me and My Tourettes
programme.desc: Documentary following three people with Tourettes syndrome as they reach turning points in their lives at a camp that could change everything for them and their families

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monacos only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanies daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi travels to the French Pyrenees, where ex-pats Mike and Joss invested everything they had into creating a holiday retreat and a beautiful family home for themselves and their two young boys. But despite the fabulous setting, the hotels bookings are poor and with their savings dwindling, the couple are desperate not to take another wrong turn. Can Alex get them back on the right track?

programme.title: The Widower
programme.desc: Factual drama based on the crimes of convicted murderer Malcolm Webster, co-written by Bafta winner Jeff Pope (Philomena). When mild-mannered nurse Malcolm (Reece Shearsmith) is confronted by his new wife over his wild spending, he starts to drug her to keep her quiet. However, a medical check-up then threatens to expose him, so he decides to silence his spouse once and for all. Three years on, he has a new bride - and history appears to be repeating itself. With Sheridan Smith and Kate Fleetwood

programme.title: McDonald and Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victims former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When the hunting den belonging to master of the hounds Gordon Radford is vandalised there is no shortage of suspects. Lord Ashfordly is seen publicly arguing with Radford, his kennelman Alec Pickering is threatened with dismissal, and farmer Richard Smith promises repercussions for the damage caused to his land. Ray Ashcroft guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the womans trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The history of modern-day mega structures, revealing that behind every seemingly impossible marvel of modern engineering is a cast of historic trailblazers

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: With the Benidorm Plaza full to bursting, and housekeeping pushed to their limits, the clock is ticking as bride-to-be Hannah and her hen party are keen to check into their rooms to get the party started. Meanwhile, restaurant manager Pepe is challenged by new hotel manager Angel to revamp his weekly Gala Dinner

programme.title: My Unique BandB
programme.desc: Simon and the team tackle a traditional Cob house built from mud in an Oxfordshire back garden for a school project and turn into a unusual BandB

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: The village prepares for Nick and Teresas wedding as the Battle of Britain draws to an end. Pat faces a big decision as Marek prepares to leave, but Bob intends to ruin her plans. Frances fights to clear her name following the closure of the factory, Alison wrestles with her conscience, and a chance encounter leaves Joyce with some unanswered questions

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new family cause panic in Aidensfield when their rabid dog goes missing, and Nick suspects Greengrass of poaching

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Dieters attempt the most hyped and written-about diets on the market today

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi faces one of her toughest challenges in Bude, Cornwall, where she sets out to help Trevor Rounding, the owner of the Atlantic House Hotel, who has a reputation for hiding away from his guests. After his partner recently stepped away from the business, former engineer Trevor has been forced front of house, but it soon becomes clear to the hotel inspector and his guests that it is not his natural habitat

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Todd manipulates Manpreet, while April is struggling and Paddy is left reeling

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah goes rummaging for evidence to aid Todds case against Theo, Ben and Eva cancel Maggies plans, Jodie takes an interest in Lilys plight, and another row with Carl triggers Debbies health

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on Adam over missing hospital funds and Mike attends a meeting to assess the mental welfare of a 50-year-old man (guest Andrew Dunn) who still lives with his mother. Elsewhere, Gordons former father-in-law pays an unexpected visit with sobering news and Ken makes a confession to his colleagues. Guest starring Michael Jayston

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Part two of two. The serial murder case founders through lack of evidence, but a psychiatrist brought in to kick-start the investigation gives the team a profile of the killer before he has time to strike again - and brings a little romance into Frosts life. Drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Susan Penhaligon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick goes undercover to investigate smuggling in Whitby, while Kate is the bearer of bad news for a girl seeking advice about abortions

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Amy and Kirsten are battling for their lives, against a ruthless armed killer, as new evidence takes the case in a different direction. Amy travels to an Air Force base in the Middle East, where she learns someone on the base was complicit in the attack. Meanwhile in Scotland, Kirsten hunts down Chapmans killer, determined not to let her pregnancy get in the way of her mission

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd struggles to get back to normality, Carl gives his decision on Ronnies offer, Jodie creates a rift in the Platt family, and Ben bulldozes Maggies efforts to downplay her past indiscretion

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jacob is being manipulated, while Marlon supports April, and Lewis hatches a plan

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: Crime drama prequel following the early career of Jane Tennison, as she starts out as a single-minded 22-year-old probationary officer in 1970s London, where chauvinism and rule-bending are the norm. Jane is thrown into a brutal murder inquiry when the body of a teenage girl, Julie-Ann Collins, is found in the underground car park of a Hackney housing estate, while the family of local crime matriarch Renee Bentley are shaken by a violent attack on her. Stefanie Martini steps into the shoes of Helen Mirren to bring the character back to the small screen, with Sam Reid, Blake Harrison and Alun Armstrong also starring

programme.title: McDonald and Dodds
programme.desc: Drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy and Walker hunt for a batch of stolen Russian icons, and suspect the victim of an assault at the Aidensfield Arms might be involved when they discover his criminal connections. Vernon embarks on another hare-brained scheme when he meets an Italian truffle salesman, while Helen is puzzled by a youngsters baffling medical history. Guest starring David Dickinson as antiques dealer Crispin Jones, with Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New broom Sgt Miller (John Duttine) introduces a rapid response car but his big city ideas receive a cool reception from the Aidensfield watch. Walker is beaten up by hoodlums but falls for nurse Clare Owen during his stay in hospital

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Walker receives a tempting offer from an old friend, but as the proposition involves a spot of rule-bending on his part, the decision to go through with it could land him in serious trouble, especially as Sgt Miller already seems less than impressed by the policemans rebellious nature. Rural drama starring, Jonathan Kerrigan, John Duttine and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert feels like he has been left with only one option, while Jacob is relieved, and Kyle goes missing

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain tries to convince Moira, while Sarah tries to comfort him, and new evidence is found

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bear makes a careless admission, while Jacob is uncomfortable and Lewis is intrigued

programme.desc: Todd manipulates Manpreet, while April is struggling and Paddy is left reeling

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jacob is being manipulated, while Marlon supports April, and Lewis hatches a plan

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets Max the setter cross, who is struggling to make any friends - either human or canine - because he is Batterseas smelliest dog. Can the vets get to the bottom of his off-putting body odour? The programme also features Ethel the cavachon, who is finding life in kennels rather overwhelming, and Milo the one-year-old Labrador, who likes nothing more than charging around after tennis balls. However, his knee ligament is damaged and unless treated, meaning he has to undergo major surgery

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a librarian, and suspicion immediately falls on two professors connected to each other by a seemingly innocent college bequest. As Endeavour and Thursday dig deeper, they uncover a web of professional and personal rivalries, and a series of broken promises with a grim connection to the Holocaust. There are further casualties in the recent series of heroin-related deaths, and Strange is convinced that a larger criminal conspiracy is at work as evidence emerges of a link to the murder of George Fancy

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: Marcus and gardener Anatoliy decide they need some advice on bee keeping after the bees mysteriously disappeared from the garden last year. The pair travel to The Newt to meet Paula for some tips. Marcus plants some pollinating flowers while Anatoliy picks some homegrown gooseberries for pickling

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Staff at Hotel Benidorm Plaza are trying to keep their cool as the reception air conditioning has a temporary malfunction, while guest experience manager Rosanna sweeps Holly and Dan off to one of Benidorms best-kept secrets. Owner Fede and his wife are turning 40 and have challenged hotel manager Angel to throw them a party at the hotel for the great and the good of Benidorm.

programme.title: Puppy Secrets: The First Six Months
programme.desc: Documentary charting the lives of four puppy litters in intimate detail, from birth to their sixth month. Owners and breeders join vet Mark Evans as he sheds light on what to expect from these young chihuahuas, sheep dogs, rescue puppies and assistance dogs as they grow into maturity. In the first of two programmes, cameras follow the pups during their first eight weeks of life, which include such milestones as opening their eyes for the first time, taking their first steps, and developing the crucial skill of bite inhibition

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson visits Langtry Manor Hotel in Bournemouth, which was built in 1877 for King Edward VII in honour of his mistress Lillie Langtry, and later turned into a hotel. Ruth immediately cites the buildings painted brickwork as off-putting, while outdated decor and imprudent budgeting add to her horror, as she points out why the business is squandering potential profits

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: When an ex-offender is found dead in gruesome circumstances, his wife claims he had turned over a new leaf, but all the evidence points to him still being up to his old tricks. As Grace and the team work to unravel what exactly killed him, Roy realises that there is a dangerous killer in Brighton with a particularly intriguing MO and must set a cunning trap before his offender disappears. Crime drama, starring John Simm

programme.title: Under Suspicion (1991)
programme.desc: A seedy private eye and his wife in 1950s Brighton make a living entrapping the partners of their married clients so their spouses can claim adultery as a grounds for divorce. However, when his wife is murdered during a scam assignation with a married American painter, he is drawn into a tortuous investigation involving the artists wife and the victims sultry mistress. Murder mystery directed by Simon Moore, with Liam Neeson, Laura San Giacomo, Maggie ONeill and Kenneth Cranham

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: Voight and Antonio are called to a murder scene to examine the body of a woman who has had one of her ears severed, a calling card trait associated with a known serial rapist in New York. News arrives of another attack - but this time the victim has survived. Meanwhile, Halstead agrees to pose as Lindsays fiance at a high school reunion, but the couple fail to make it to the event and end up spending an intimate night in a bar. Crossover episode with Law and Order: Special Victims Unit, starring Jason Baghe, Jon Seda, Ice-T and Kelli Giddish

programme.title: McDonald and Dodds
programme.sub-title: We Need to Talk About Doreen
programme.desc: A birthday weekend away goes devastatingly wrong when Angela and four of her friends travel down from Glasgow to celebrate in Bath. When a rugby player they met during the course of the night turns up dead, they all become suspects as McDonald and Dodds investigate what appears to be a case of murder. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances by Sharon Rooney, John Thomson and Natalie Gumede

programme.title: Spreadsheet
programme.desc: Lauren receives a rather questionable anonymous picture, and sets out on a mission with Alex to entice the sender out of the shadows - but their sleuthing backfires when she inadvertently posts her own private photo online. Katherine Parkinson and Rowan Witt star

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of Edith Negus from Bridlington, East Yorkshire. Edith died in October 2014 at the age of 102, but it was only after her death that her family discovered she was conned out of nearly £290,000 by Julie Sayles, her carer. Sayles bought two houses with money she took from a bank account she shared with Edith, and after being found out, was jailed for nine years in June 2017

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A final selection of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are Michael McIntyre, Olivia Colman, David Beckham, Rebel Wilson, Jason Derulo, Sylvester Stallone, Michael B Jordan, Katherine Ryan, Will Ferrell, Demi Lovato, Sacha Baron Cohen, Ashley Banjo and Patrick Dempsey

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the murder of Julie Archer by her brother Stephen, who set fire to her in her Greater Manchester home in 2016. Police had their chief suspect almost immediately, but Stephen went on the run, posing an enormous danger to the public at large

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Liam, Sam, Sarah and Kiya

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Antony from Manchester, Catherine from New Malden, Heidi from Rochester and Charlie, originally from Johannesburg, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show which takes place in public locations around the UK. He invites passers-by in Leeds to take part in a series of unusual challenges, with a prize of £1,000 up for grabs

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: In this edition, Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from Liverpool, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Candice, Esther, Craig and Shaun vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Brian, Elmo and Michael compete for the affections of Lauren from Milton Keynes. Which of the three single shoppers fashion choices will win them a date?

programme.title: Storage Hoarders
programme.desc: Aggie MacKenzie has a real challenge on her hands when facing French resistance trying to convince goth Carole to get rid of her clutter when she simply cannot let go. She also tries to help domestic goddess Debbie to clean up and clear out

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Marilyn, a singer from Derbyshire, explores three mens homes in the hope of finding a suitable partner - and her sister is determined to make her expand her horizons and look for a more manly prospect. She may have come to the wrong places, when she encounters a passion for music, a love heart-themed decor and a caravan. Narrated by Rochelle Humes

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Sasha, a 24-year-old ex-footballer and single mother from Preston looks for love with the help of her best friend Kayla, picking through the homes of a skateboarding fan, a builder, and a man with colourful hobbies for evidence they may be the ambitious, DIY-loving family man she is looking for Narrated by Rochelle Humes

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
